frameworks.name: statistical confidence (probability-correct)
frameworks.one_line: Confidence as posterior probability of being correct given choice and evidence

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Dynamic temporal integration of noisy evidence toward a decision

frameworks.name: signal-detection / diagnostic signatures of confidence
frameworks.one_line: Three canonical signatures including opposite evidence-strength dependence on correct vs error trials

core_question: How do diagnostic signatures of decision confidence evolve over time within an evidence accumulation process?
key_assumptions: Confidence equals the probability of being correct given choice and evidence
Evidence is integrated dynamically over time toward a choice
Static probability-correct framework can be extended to time-varying accumulated evidence
manipulations: Query timing of confidence judgments across different points in time
Choice correctness compared (correct vs error trials)
key_findings: Dynamic model predicts diagnostic confidence signatures depend on time
Predicted stronger opposite dependence of confidence on evidence strength across correct vs error trials as time progresses
Human behavior confirmed these time-dependent predictions during random dot motion discrimination
interpretations: Human confidence reports reflect dynamics of probability-correct given accumulated evidence and choice
Static probability-correct framework is incomplete without a temporal accumulation component
